text:
Once there was a dog named Rover who loved collecting acorns. Every day after school, he would trot over to the oak trees by the park and gather a bounty of shiny acorns. He had a particular favorite tree where he'd sit and just listen to the rustle of leaves carrying sounds from the neighborhood. Rover had the knack for timing—not too early, not too late—for the perfect acorn drop.

One autumn, Rover was walking home when he heard a soft tapping sound. Peering around the edge of a bush, he saw a little bat flying toward him. It had wings like the acorns he loved so much!

The bat landed on his paw. "Hello," it chirped warmly. "Where did you find these?"

"Oh, lots of them," Rover replied with a giggle. "Why do you ask?"

The bat twiddled one wing. "Oh, I just like acorns. I've got a big collection stored away in my cave."
